title: Compare decimals visually
description: Practice comparing decimals with the help of visual aids.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare the decimals with $>,<,$ or $=$.**  
*Hint: Think about how you would fill in each grid below to help you compare the decimals.*

$0.17$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] $0.2$

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]
hints: $\green{0.17}$ covers **less** area than $\blue{0.2}$, so $\green{0.17}$ is **less** than $\blue{0.2}$.

options.alt: A big square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. In all 10 rows, all 10 squares are not shaded.
A big square is divided into 10 equal horizontal rectangles. All 10 rectangles are not shaded.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Compare the decimals with $>,<,$ or $=$.**  
*Hint: Think about where each decimal is located on the number line below to help you compare.*


$4.4$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] $4.28$




[[☃ image 1]]
hints: $\green{4.4}$ is located to the **right** of $\blue{4.28}$ on the number line, so $\green{4.4}$ is **greater** than $\blue{4.28}$.

options.alt: A number line from 4.2  to 4.5 with tick marks at each one-hundredth unit.

hints: $\greenD5\greenD{\text{ ones}}$ and $\greenD{34}\greenD{\text{ hundredths}}$ is located to the **right** of $\blueD{5.2}$ on the number line, so $\greenD5\greenD{\text{ ones}}$ and $\greenD{34}\greenD{\text{ hundredths}}$ is **greater** than $\blueD{5.2}$.

options.alt: A number line labeled 5.1 to 5.4 with tick marks at every one-hundredth unit.
